campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans for a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of temporary ordinance #2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for bridge planning and feasibility study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get feedback from residents about a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with the ordinance by skipping the standard first reading step and planning to vote on final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a proposed ordinance
Council waived the first reading requirement to speed up the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at the next regular council meeting
Details of the specific ordinance topic not specified in agenda

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to wrap up their 2026 weed control program, establish new rules for how the council operates, and address affordable housing policies. The council also approved raising two flags—Progress Pride and Juneteenth—and heard from the regional transit authority about transportation.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program
New affordable housing rules for below-market-rate units
Official council operating procedures adopted
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
Regional transit authority (VTA) update

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with ongoing projects and services. The council authorized the town manager to sign deals with various companies for construction work, professional services, and insurance coverage, plus approved joining a statewide insurance group to help manage the city's risk and costs.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Multiple amendments to existing agreements with contractors and service providers
Art transfer agreement authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving its young people and families. They discussed progress on youth programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services, checked in on library improvements, and reviewed code enforcement activity.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer programs for kids
Youth Intervention Services annual performance review
Library facility upgrades and customer experience improvements
Code Enforcement annual report and activity
Progress on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in-person and online. The council provided instructions for residents who wanted to speak during the meeting, including how to join the Zoom call and raise their hand to comment.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom for residents
Public comment procedures for online participants
Hybrid in-person and remote meeting format

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their April meeting where they heard personal stories from three commissioners about their connections to the community. The group approved meeting notes from their March gathering and conducted a flag salute to start the evening.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission regular business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a presentation about a food and budget program, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from March and opened the floor for residents to speak about local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization
Summer job opportunities for young people
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 using both in-person and online options so residents could participate however they preferred. The council is making it easy for community members to join and share their thoughts either in person or remotely through Zoom.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation via Zoom with meeting ID 997-0675-9306
Phone dial-in option for those without video access
Written public comments accepted through eComment system on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to approve several administrative and infrastructure matters. Key decisions included joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a local educational organization, awarding a construction contract, and reviewing updates to the city's housing and community development plans.
keyTopics: Tax Board partnership with County
Fee waiver request from Milpitas Community Educational
Construction contract award and project approval
Housing and community development plan updates
New council workplan item requests

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for 2027. The main decision involved reviewing financial forecasts, moving money between accounts, and adjusting how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates going up for residential customers (G-1), apartment buildings and small businesses (G-2), and large commercial users (G-3)
Approval of the city's gas utility financial plan and budget reserves for fiscal year 2027
Transfer of funds between the gas utility account and the city's general operating budget
